description: Scientific pre-registration v8 (P1.2-F). Minimal fix: RESTORES the top-level `tolerances` block {sync_threshold_E12: 0.02, sync_tail_frac: 0.25} that existed in prereg v3 and was accidentally dropped when prereg v4 was created (never declared as a scientific change). run_g0a_exact_v2 and run_g0b_calibrated_v2 read ctx.prereg['tolerances']; without it run_suite_v2.py --plan raised KeyError, so freeze v7 was NON-EXECUTABLE (docs/audits/p1_2e_independent_audit.md). This restoration changes NO seed, grid, estimand, gate, inference or decision rule and reinstates values already frozen in v3. NO real data. NOT EXECUTED beyond the authorized one-shot cheap suite.

lineage.reason_for_v8: execution freeze v7 (tag switchsync-synthetic-execution-v7-freeze, 61a63f3) is SUPERSEDED / NONEXECUTABLE / NEVER RUN: it lacked the `tolerances` block, so --plan raised KeyError. Only the accidentally-dropped tolerances block is restored; no other scientific field changed.

inference_contract.single_shared_function: src/metrics/inference.py:paired_decision (used identically by G1_weak, G1_strict, G2, G3, G4-baseline)
inference_contract.std_definition: sample standard deviation of the paired differences, ddof=1
inference_contract.zero_handling: |d|<=1e-12 dropped from the sign test and reported (n_zero)
inference_contract.decision: p>=alpha -> INCONCLUSIVE(NOT_SIGNIFICANT); elif mean>band -> PASS; elif mean<-band -> FAIL; else INCONCLUSIVE(TIE)
inference_contract.corrected_fact: an exact two-sided sign test with 8/8 equal signs gives p = 2*C(8,0)/2^8 = 0.0078125 (< 0.05). 8 evaluation seeds CAN reach significance.
inference_contract.bootstrap_role: DESCRIPTOR ONLY (n_boot=2000, boot_seed=20260713), not part of the decision; small-n caveat recorded. The rule is the exact sign test + band, NOT a bootstrap CI.

G0A_exact_reproduction.goal: Reproduce the paper regime (sigma_12=0.1) at paper scale.

cost_rule.isolated_layer_chaos_prerequisite: lambda_max>0 at the same (size, seed) before the switching cells of that size may count
cost_rule.pass_quantifier: PASS iff, for EVERY deciding size, ALL fast cells (T_swt<=25) have frac_synced>=0.5 AND ALL slow cells (T_swt>=120) have frac_synced<0.5, with the chaos prerequisite met; a single favorable N=100 (non-deciding) cell can NEVER decide the gate
cost_rule.partial_rule: an incomplete deciding cell is INCONCLUSIVE(INCONCLUSIVE_BY_COST) ONLY when (a) a CLEAN frozen-deadline expiry was recorded (interrupted_by_cost=True) AND (b) NO invalidating technical cause exists; an incomplete cell for ANY OTHER cause (missing without a deadline, >20% technical failures, or zero successful seeds in an observable required cell) is EXECUTION_INVALID. See failure_precedence.
cost_rule.checkpoint: durable, append-only, OUTSIDE the repo: one atomic entry per cell (flush+fsync), keyed by freeze/prereg/contract/runner SHAs and params; duplicates rejected; the checkpoint is EVIDENCE of what ran, NOT a resume input; no cell may be deleted to obtain a favorable verdict
cost_rule.deadline_scope: a SOFT wall-time budget (time.monotonic) bounds the chaos prerequisite and the switching simulations; it is checked before each size, before each seed/cell, and DURING chaos (chunked Benettin) and switching (chunked RK4). It is SOFT, not hard: a non-interruptible fsync or the final checkpoint hashing may overshoot it, so elapsed time and any overshoot are recorded, not assumed away
cost_rule.size_order: deciding sizes {200,400} run FIRST; N=100 may not consume budget before them
cost_rule.clean_deadline_conversion: ONLY a DeadlineExceeded/LyapunovDeadlineExceeded from the frozen budget check (interrupted_by_cost=True), AND only in the absence of any invalidating technical cause (see failure_precedence), converts missing cells to INCONCLUSIVE_BY_COST; a missing cell WITHOUT interrupted_by_cost, or any >20%/zero-success technical failure, is EXECUTION_INVALID, never cost
cost_rule.checkpoint_ledger: hash-chained append-only JSONL outside the repo: per-record sequence number, prev_record_hash, record_hash; load verifies the full chain; duplicate cell UIDs, sequence gaps, reordering, changed results and foreign provenance are rejected; a truncated final line is detected and the valid prefix is preserved as EVIDENCE ONLY; there is NO continuation and NO resume from it — a new run needs a new authorization token and starts from scratch; evidence is never silently deleted
cost_rule.state_record: the INTERRUPTED_BY_COST checkpoint state record is evidence only and is never counted as a completed scientific cell
cost_rule.chaos_deadline: the chunked deadline abort is applied to the chaos/Benettin computation as well as the switching integration
cost_rule.checkpoint_relative_paths: the G0A report records the checkpoint by RELATIVE name + sha256, never an absolute host path
cost_rule.failure_precedence: FROZEN, highest first: (1) corrupt/invalid contract or checkpoint -> EXECUTION_INVALID; (2) >20% technical failures OR zero successful seeds in ANY observable required cell -> EXECUTION_INVALID (this OUTRANKS cost: a recorded technical failure is NEVER converted to INCONCLUSIVE_BY_COST, because the cost paths re-raise before a failed record is written); (3) otherwise, required cells missing under a clean deadline -> INCONCLUSIVE(INCONCLUSIVE_BY_COST); (4) required cells missing WITHOUT a deadline -> EXECUTION_INVALID; (5) complete grid -> PASS/FAIL.
cost_rule.counters: n_science_cells EXCLUDES the kind=state record; the report discloses n_ledger_records, n_science_cells and n_state_records separately, plus soft_deadline_seconds, elapsed_seconds and overshoot_seconds

G0B_calibrated_demonstration.label: CALIBRATED_QUALITATIVE_DEMONSTRATION (NOT reproduction)
G0B_calibrated_demonstration.accept: DEMONSTRATED(=PASS) iff ALL fast cells (T_swt<=10) synchronize (frac_synced>=0.5) AND ALL slow cells (T_swt>=160) do NOT (frac_synced<0.5); >20% failed/nonfinite seeds in any cell -> EXECUTION_INVALID
G0B_calibrated_demonstration.forbidden_claim: no N-dependence of the switching threshold without a multi-N switching grid

G0C_msf_minimal.goal: Minimal transverse Lyapunov, anti-phase channels only.
G0C_msf_minimal.accept: channels not anti-phase -> EXECUTION_INVALID(PREREQ_FAIL); no Psi<0 at the fastest T_swt -> FAIL; Psi<0 but onset independent of T_swt -> INCONCLUSIVE(PARTIAL_NO_TSWT_DEPENDENCE); Psi<0 with T_swt-dependent onset -> PASS

G1_weak.goal: Switching beats static-sparse of equal instantaneous density.
G1_weak.estimand: gamma_selected_arm - gamma_static_sparse, paired over evaluation seeds
G1_weak.arm_selection: the switching RATE arm is chosen on the paired_selection seeds ONLY (max mean selection gamma; deterministic tie-break by canonical arm order fast<intermediate<slow) and FROZEN before any evaluation seed is opened

G1_strict.goal: Switching beats the average graph AND the best-of-frozen-candidate-set static.
G1_strict.comparator_note: the admissible universe is C(24,6)=134596 subsets; the frozen candidate set is the base snapshots UNION a frozen random search (best_static_search). No exhaustive/certified search is performed, so G1_strict MUST NOT claim to beat the best admissible static.
G1_strict.estimand: gamma_selected_arm - max(gamma_average, gamma_best_of_frozen_candidates), paired over evaluation seeds; candidate static SUBSET selected on selection seeds and FROZEN
G1_strict.candidate_ordering: candidates are canonical sorted tuples; ties in selection score broken lexicographically
G1_strict.interpretability: NOT_INTERPRETABLE unless G1_weak PASS (diagnostic value may be computed but not interpreted as a gate)

G2_order.goal: Temporal order changes contraction beyond the multiset/average.
G2_order.estimand: delta_s = gamma_ordered - median(gamma_permutations); aggregated by the shared paired_decision over evaluation seeds
G2_order.decision: PASS iff shared paired_decision(delta_s) is PASS or FAIL (a significant order effect in either direction beyond the band); else INCONCLUSIVE (evidence favors aggregate connectivity)
G2_order.not_abs_vs_zero: the statistic is the signed delta against the permutation MEDIAN, aggregated by a paired test; NOT abs(effect) compared to zero as a null mean
G2_order.method_definition: per evaluation seed: delta_s = gamma_ordered - median(gamma over g2_n_perm frozen order-permutations with independent per-seed RNG); the delta_s are aggregated across seeds by the shared exact sign test + effect band. This is NOT a per-seed permutation test: it detects a systematic displacement of the observed order relative to the permutation median, and order effects of varying sign across seeds can cancel. This limitation is frozen and disclosed.

G3_robustness.goal: Robustness of the G1_weak advantage to IMPLEMENTED stresses.
G3_robustness.per_stage: shared paired_decision on (gamma_fast - gamma_static) over the stage seed block
G3_robustness.gate_rule: PASS iff BOTH faithful and mild_heterogeneity stages are PASS; else INCONCLUSIVE(LIMITED). A signed stage where ANY seed lacks a strictly negative off-diagonal weight, OR whose off-diagonal Frobenius budget differs from its unsigned comparator by more than signed_budget_tolerance, -> EXECUTION_INVALID(STRESS_NOT_IMPLEMENTED)
G3_robustness.per_seed_metadata: operator metadata recorded for ALL seeds (not only the last)

G4_identifiability.goal: Recover switching from observations, past-only, same-realization ground truth.
G4_identifiability.horizon_assertion: for every seed, schedule.total_steps MUST equal horizon_steps (asserted at runtime before simulating)
conditions: basis estimator beats the factor-confounded baseline
G4_identifiability.beats_baseline: per-seed paired difference (basis_precision - levelcorr_precision) fed to the shared paired_decision with floor=identifiability_margin (0.05); PASS required (significant positive margin)
G4_identifiability.accept: PASS iff ALL FOUR conditions hold; else FAIL
G4_identifiability.async_language: asynchrony/LOCF (Epps-like) degradation, NOT a causal Epps demonstration

custody.run_dir: EXTERNAL to the repo, required for a full run; results NEVER inside the frozen repo
custody.campaign_id: sha256(prereg_canonical | exec_canonical | freeze_content | freeze_commit)[:16] — the scientific campaign identity
custody.no_overwrite_resume_retry: a target report that already exists is refused; there is NO resume and NO retry: a .staging (once its live flock is gone), .interrupted or .invalid attempt is TERMINAL, and a new run needs a NEW authorization token (a different attempt_id) and starts from scratch
custody.suite_transaction: the suite writes to a staging dir and publishes to <run-dir>/<attempt_id>/ by a SINGLE atomic rename ONLY on full success after the pre-rename validation passes; on a gate-invalid result OR any custody failure it writes a failure ledger, preserves staging as .interrupted, and publishes NO success bundle
custody.sequential_individual_runs: individual runners may write distinct reports into the same <run-dir>/<attempt_id>/ sequentially; the first output never invalidates the freeze identity (outputs are external)
custody.flag_semantics: --cheap-only runs G0B/G0C/G1G2/G3/G4 and leaves G0A NOT_RUN; --include-g0a-expensive adds G0A; supplying BOTH is a contradiction and is rejected

removed_gates.H3: coverage-vs-reachability isolation removed; temporal_reachability_ratio is a descriptor only

identity_and_custody.campaign_id: sha256(prereg_canonical | execution_contract_canonical | freeze_content_hash | freeze_commit)[:16] — identifies the scientific contract
identity_and_custody.attempt_id: sha256(campaign_id | scope | token_sha)[:16], token_sha = sha256(token_utf8) — recomputable by an auditor from the recorded authorization_token_sha256 WITHOUT the raw token
identity_and_custody.scope_is_code_determined: the scope is derived from the invocation (runner module or suite flags), never operator-supplied text
identity_and_custody.authorization_token: supplied with --authorization-token; rejected if empty/whitespace/non-string; recorded ONLY as sha256; attempt_id derives from token_sha, not the raw token
identity_and_custody.provenance_rule: each report records the SHA-256 of the scientific runner file that computed it; the orchestrator SHA is set exclusively by run_suite_v2 via child_context() (immutable per-gate contexts); there is NO CLI argument to inject an orchestrator SHA; individual runners record orchestrator_sha256=null and execution_mode='individual:<gate>'
identity_and_custody.attempt_directories: <run-dir>/<attempt_id>.staging (in-progress, flock-held) | .failed (failure ledger) | .interrupted (terminal) | .invalid (terminal; an exceptional post-rename verification failure) | <attempt_id>/ (final, immutable, SEALED)
identity_and_custody.attempt_manifest: integral inventory of ALL artifacts (report, G0A checkpoint, evidence) each with size + sha256 + role; plus campaign/attempt ids, scope, document hashes, HEAD/tag, STRUCTURED command (interpreter+version, normalized argv with token masked as <sha256:...>, hashes/flags, scope, resolved run-dir), runner+orchestrator SHAs, authorization_token_sha256, UTC start/end, exit status, gate verdicts, environment, state=COMPLETED, and the manifest's own content hash
identity_and_custody.sealing: a SINGLE manifest-and-staging validation function runs BEFORE the rename and checks: manifest dict shape and field types; the recomputed manifest content hash; campaign/attempt/scope coherence; that artifacts is a dict of safely-named, non-reserved entries each with size(int>=0) + valid sha256 + valid role; that staging is FLAT (no subdirectory, symlink, FIFO/socket/device) and contains EXACTLY the declared artifacts plus attempt_manifest.json and SEALED; that each artifact's bytes match its inventoried size and hash; and that SEALED equals the manifest content hash. Sequence: inventory staging -> build manifest -> write manifest + SEALED INTO staging -> fsync files and directory -> run the full validation (including manifest+SEALED) -> ONLY if every check passes, a SINGLE atomic rename staging->final (no final-without-SEAL window) -> post-rename re-verification. A deterministic pre-rename validation error leaves `final` NONEXISTENT; if the exceptional post-rename re-verification fails, the directory is moved to <attempt_id>.invalid and NEVER left as a successful attempt. verify_sealed_attempt NEVER raises on a malformed manifest — it returns ok=False with structured errors.
identity_and_custody.concurrency: an exclusive flock on <run-dir>/<attempt_id>.lock prevents concurrent executions of the same attempt_id
identity_and_custody.coexistence: cheap-suite and g0a-only attempts coexist under the same campaign_id with different attempt_ids; a published bundle is immutable and no individual runner may add files to it
identity_and_custody.campaign_result_bundle: a campaign bundle is a NEW directory containing a campaign_manifest.json that REFERENCES the sealed attempts by their attempt_id and manifest_content_hash (bytes of the referenced attempts are never modified); its construction is a separate, future authorized step
identity_and_custody.valid_attempt_contents: exactly attempt_manifest.json, SEALED, and the inventoried artifacts — nothing else

crash_and_resume_policy.no_auto_retry: a crash or interruption is NEVER retried
crash_and_resume_policy.interrupted_is_terminal: the staging directory is preserved as <attempt_id>.interrupted and is TERMINAL: it is never resumed or mixed
crash_and_resume_policy.new_run_from_scratch: a subsequent execution requires a NEW authorization token, yields a DIFFERENT attempt_id, and starts from scratch
crash_and_resume_policy.no_resume_feature: there is NO resume command or argument; the checkpoint is evidence and an instrument of a LIVE run or a clean cost-termination, and never authorizes a resume
crash_and_resume_policy.technical_failure_is_not_science: a technical failure (crash, external signal, resource kill) never becomes a scientific verdict; only a CLEAN frozen-deadline expiry may convert to INCONCLUSIVE_BY_COST
crash_and_resume_policy.incomplete_cause_distinction: an incomplete run is INCONCLUSIVE_BY_COST only under a CLEAN frozen-deadline expiry AND no invalidating technical cause; incomplete for any other cause (missing without a deadline, >20% technical failures, zero successful seeds) is EXECUTION_INVALID
crash_and_resume_policy.terminal_states: .staging (only while a live run holds the flock), .interrupted and .invalid are ALL terminal; none is ever resumed or mixed, and no checkpoint ever authorizes a resume

capture_per_seed: sanitized message (truncated, no traceback, repo paths masked)
failed_seed_policy.under_or_equal_20_percent: metrics are computed over SUCCESSFUL seeds only (the denominator excludes failed seeds); the inferential n is the number of successful seeds; the failures and reduced n MUST be disclosed in the report
failed_seed_policy.g0b_frac_synced_denominator: SUCCESSFUL_SEEDS_ONLY (frozen choice; failed seeds disclosed per cell, never counted as non-synchronization)
failed_seed_policy.g0c_single_seed: G0C has one seed: ANY technical failure or nonfinite value => EXECUTION_INVALID
failed_seed_policy.g0a_distinctions: G0A distinguishes, in FROZEN precedence order: a technical failed cell (recorded with failed=true; >20% or zero-success in an observable required cell => EXECUTION_INVALID, OUTRANKING cost), incomplete-by-clean-frozen-cost (clean deadline AND no invalidating cause => INTERRUPTED_BY_COST => INCONCLUSIVE_BY_COST), and external interruption (propagates; staging preserved as .interrupted; NO scientific verdict)

enforcement.g1_g2: failures captured during BOTH selection and evaluation; a COMMON mask of complete selection seeds is used for arm AND best-static selection; >20% selection or >20% evaluation failures => EXECUTION_INVALID
enforcement.g0b: full failure records (not integer seed lists); ordinary exceptions and nonfinite captured; KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit propagate as external interruptions
enforcement.g0c: single seed: any exception OR nonfinite => EXECUTION_INVALID with a full failure record
enforcement.g4: >20% applied per configured async variant; ALL failure records aggregated into provenance
enforcement.g0a: failed cell recorded as failed=true; FROZEN precedence (see gates.G0A_exact_reproduction.cost_rule.failure_precedence): >20% technical failures OR zero successful seeds in an observable required cell => EXECUTION_INVALID and this OUTRANKS cost; missing-without-deadline => EXECUTION_INVALID; ONLY missing-under-clean-deadline with no invalidating cause => INCONCLUSIVE_BY_COST; the INTERRUPTED_BY_COST state record is NOT a completed science cell; n_science_cells excludes kind=state and n_ledger_records / n_science_cells / n_state_records are reported separately
enforcement.record_structure: every failure record: {exception_type, sanitized message (truncated, repo paths masked, no traceback), seed, cell, timestamp_utc}; the report schema validates this structure
